{{Quote|Empress Teta forces, this is ''Shadow Runner'' on command-coded frequency. We're making an emergency run to drop those urgent supplies–watch your heads!|Timar Daragon|<ref name="conquest" />}}
The '''''Shadow Runner''''' was the personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of husband-and-wife [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Hok Daragon|Hok]] and [[Timar Daragon]]. In the year [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] broke out, and the forces of [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] were [[Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)|encircled and besieged]] by rebels on [[Kirrek]]. Seeing an opportunity to make a profit by running supplies to the Tetan [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] on Kirrek, Hok and Timar Daragon got the ''Shadow Runner'' repaired by [[Aarrba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] on [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] and set out for Kirrek. However, by the time the Daragons reached Kirrek the battle had changed; usage of the exotic [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] known as [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Memit Nadill]] and [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] had released the last reserves of desperation in the rebels, who began firing on the weakest targets in the sky. Encumbered by the weight of the cargo, the ''Shadow Runner'' was not able to enter into sufficient evasive action, and the ship was caught in a crossfire and destroyed, killing the Daragons.

==Description==
{{Quote|Can't maneuver well with all that heavy cargo aboard!|Hok Daragon|<ref name="conquest" />}}
The ''Shadow Runner'' was [[Color/Legends|colored purple]], and had several tendril-like apparatuses protruding out of one end of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. On the top of the [[Hull/Legends|hull]], the ship featured several wings. The ''Shadow Runner'' featured a ramp coming out of its midsection, allowing goods and supplies to be loaded onto the cargo hold of the ship. However, too much weight in the cargo hold would cause the ship to slow down and its maneuverability to decrease. The ''Shadow Runner'' also featured battle-ready [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], although they could be quickly damaged by steady [[Turret/Legends|turret]] [[Laser/Legends|fire]]. The [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] was located around the midsection of the ship, with its [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] protruding from the main body of the vessel. The [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] contained the [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] controls, as well as a command-coded [[Comm unit/Legends|comm system]].<ref name="conquest" />

==History==
{{Quote|We'll be in and out before they can blink their eyes.|Hok Daragon|<ref name="conquest" />}}
[[File:ShadowRunner1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Shadow Runner in flight]]
The ''Shadow Runner'' was the personal starship of husband-and-wife ship operators [[Hok Daragon|Hok]] and [[Timar Daragon]], natives of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. The Koros [[Star system/Legends|system]] erupted into [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|war]] as the [[Politics/Legends|political]] leader [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] launched a campaign to unify the seven [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the system. Her forces succeeded in unifying the first six, but the campaign stalled at [[Kirrek]], where an [[Army/Legends|army]] of rebels and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] [[Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)|encircled and besieged]] a group of Teta's [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the amount of supplies accessible to the besieged soldiers dwindled greatly. Seeing an opportunity to make a profit, Hok and Timar Daragon went to [[Aarrba's Repair Dock]], a repair yard on [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] owned by a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] named [[Aarrba]], to get the ''Shadow Runner'' [[Realspace/Legends|spaceworthy]] so that they could run supplies to the Tetan soldiers. Aarrba fixed the ''Shadow Runner'' in exchange for a portion of the Daragons' profits, and also kept their backup ship, the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', as collateral until they returned.<ref name="conquest" />

The ''Shadow Runner'' promptly had its cargo hold filled to the brim with supplies by [[Droid/Legends|droids]] at Aarrba's Repair Dock. Hok and Timar Daragon bade their children, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], farewell and departed in the ''Shadow Runner''. However, by the time the ''Shadow Runner'' reached Kirrek, the dynamic of the battle had changed greatly. Teta had enlisted two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Memit Nadill]] and [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], to lead her forces. The two Jedi utilized the exotic [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] known as [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to lower the morale of the rebels, unleashing their last reserves of desperation. Looking to do as much damage as they could before their defeat, the rebels began firing on the weakest targets in the sky. The ''Shadow Runner'', upon entering Kirrek's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], was set upon by the rebel guns, forcing the Daragons to take emergency evasive action. Unfortunately for the Daragons, the heavy cargo aboard the ship reduced the ''Shadow Runner''{{'s}} maneuverability. Caught in a crossfire, the ''Shadow Runner'' was destroyed and Hok and Timar Daragon were killed.<ref name="conquest" />

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Shadow Runner'' was owned and operated by husband-and-wife pilots Hok and Timar Daragon. The Daragons were killed during the Battle of Kirrek, however, and the ''Shadow Runner'' was destroyed.<ref name="conquest" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Shadow Runner''{{'s}} only appearance in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] is in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. This appearance comes in the prologue issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Conquest and Unification'']], in which the ship is destroyed. In this issue the ship was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="conquest" />
